Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Jerry Threet, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a Municipal Corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco.
No appearance by defendants Ed Pope, Ed Pope, Inc., a California corporation, or Club Paree, LLC.
Defaults were entered against said defendants on November 5, 2009
Doe defendants were dismissed today, November 17, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a Municipal Corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco, and against the Defaulted Defendants Ed Pope, Ed Pope, Inc. a California corporation, and Club Paree, LLC as follows:

Civil Penalties - $750,000.00.
Attorney Fees - Reserved.
Costs - Reserved, for a total judgment in the amount of $750,000.00.

Additionally, it is further Ordered that the business operated by defendants Ed Pope, Ed Pope, Inc., a California corporation, and Club Paree, LLC, located @ 220 Jones Street, San Francisco, CA, is hereby closed in order to abate the ongoing public nuisance at said location, and that the above-mentioned defendants are enjoined from operating a business of a similar nature at the property.

This Judgment is stayed for a period of seven (7) days to allow the parties to finalize a written version of the proposed Stipulated Judgment attached as Exhibit A to the Judgment.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulated Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JERRY THREET, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3914 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION AND THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Enforce Stipulated Injunction IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
